title: Debugging
description: Find and fix the bug — console.log, stack traces, and the common JS pitfalls.
objectives: Use console.log to inspect values mid-function
Read code top-down looking for the gap between intent and behavior
Recognize common JS bug patterns when you meet one
recap: Bugs are the gap between what you wrote and what you meant. The move you used in this lesson — print the variable you suspect, watch how it actually changes — works every time. You also met four bug shapes that show up over and over: a wrong initial value (the main task), an off-by-one (`lastItem`), a missing `return` (`countEvens`), and the silent reassignment-vs-discard trap (`greetEveryone`). The fastest coders aren't the ones who don't write bugs — they're the ones who recognize the shape and fix it in 30 seconds.
practicePrompts: Take any function you wrote in an earlier lesson and add a console.log inside its loop. What changes when the input changes?
Throw an error on purpose: try calling a method that doesn't exist (e.g. `"hi".reverse()` — which doesn't exist on strings). Read what the message says.

practiceExercises.title: Fix: lastItem returns undefined
practiceExercises.prompt: The function `lastItem(arr)` is supposed to return the last element of the array. Right now it returns `undefined` for any non-empty array. The body is one line — but that one line is wrong. Fix it.
practiceExercises.goal: Notice that the LAST index is `length - 1`, not `length`.
hints: Try `console.log(arr.length)` for an array like `[1, 2, 3]`. What does it print?
Array indexes start at 0. So the LAST index is one less than something you already know.

practiceExercises.title: Fix: countEvens always returns undefined
practiceExercises.prompt: The function `countEvens(numbers)` should return how many even numbers are in the array. Right now every test gets `undefined`. The loop runs and counts correctly, yet tests still fail.
practiceExercises.goal: Notice the bug between 'computed correctly' and 'handed back'.
hints: After the loop ends, what does the function actually hand back to its caller?
A function with no explicit `return` returns `undefined`. The tests are inspecting whatever the function hands back.
Add `return count;` as the last line of the function body.

practiceExercises.title: Fix: greetEveryone returns an empty string
practiceExercises.prompt: The function `greetEveryone(names)` should return one long string with greetings for every name. Right now it always returns `""`. The loop runs and the line concatenates — but the result keeps disappearing. Fix it.
practiceExercises.goal: Spot the silent reassignment-vs-discard bug — `a + b` doesn't change `a`; `a += b` does.
hints: The loop SHOULD be growing `greeting` each iteration. Is it actually growing? How would you check?
`greeting + "..."` builds a new string but doesn't save it anywhere. You need to put the new string back into `greeting`.
`greeting += "Hello, " + name + "! ";`  — the `+=` is the assignment that was missing.
